bodyTail:
//github.com/lequoctran181/IMC_Challenge_2026 .

Hard submission requirements:
- single self-contained C++17 source file, no commits, no external nonstandard dependencies except standard library/Eigen if absolutely included by judge;
- compile with: g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -pipe;
- official sample must output first line exactly: 8 12;
- output must be valid closed 2-manifold, non-degenerate, vertex-Hausdorff valid, <= original vertex count, <=100 MiB;
- avoid candidates that merely reproduce the 81.93 plateau, low-score 16-23, or sample first line 9 14.

Think broadly and implement, not just discuss: infer hidden test structure, exploit vertex-only Hausdorff, build recognizers, case-specific remeshing, parameterized families, or any legal structural breakthrough. Return only when you have a ready-to-submit submission_next.cpp file plus complete fallback code. If your UI cannot attach a file, say so explicitly and put the entire code block, but first try hard to provide the file.

Show more
